title: Crispy Citrus Kenyan Potato Dumplings
description: Citrusy, crispy dumplings made from potatoes, dill pickles, and bright citrus zest—an egg‑free nostalgic Kenyan dish that satisfies breakfast, lunch, or snack cravings.

ingredients.name: Celery root
ingredients.notes: finely diced, optional for extra flavor

instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine the cubed potatoes and diced celery root, tossing to coat evenly with salt and pepper.
2. Add the ground dill weed, chopped dill pickles, lemon zest, orange zest, lemon juice, and orange juice to the potatoes, mixing thoroughly until all ingredients are well incorporated.
3. Heat the olive oil in a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat, ensuring the oil coats the bottom of the pan.
4. Using a spoon or small ice‑cream scoop, form the potato mixture into bite‑size balls and place them gently into the hot oil, cooking in batches to avoid overcrowding.
5. Fry the dumplings for 3–4 minutes per side, or until each side turns a luscious golden‑brown and develops a crisp exterior.
6. Transfer the cooked dumplings to a paper‑towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
7. Repeat the frying process with the remaining dough until all dumplings are cooked.
8. Sprinkle the crispy dumplings with freshly chopped parsley for a burst of color and herbal freshness.
9. Serve immediately, optionally accompanied by a light cucumber or tomato salad for a complete Kenyan meal.
10. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days; reheat in a hot skillet for a minute to restore crispness.
